content:
World-Class MemorAI MCP Enhancement Plan - Complete Implementation Roadmap Saved

PLAN OVERVIEW:
• Document: WORLD_CLASS_ENHANCEMENT_PLAN.md created with comprehensive 24-week implementation plan
• 3 Strategic Phases: Foundation Enhancements (8 weeks), Enterprise Features (8 weeks), World-Class Innovation (8 weeks)
• 12 Major Feature Categories: Memory relationships, advanced search, rich types, batch operations, analytics, performance, security, AI intelligence, conversation interface, ecosystem integrations
• Success Metrics: Defined for each phase with specific performance targets
• Resource Requirements: Team size, infrastructure costs, technology stack specified
• Competitive Positioning: Analysis of market leaders and our advantages

PHASE 1 HIGHLIGHTS (Weeks 1-8):
• Memory Relationships & Graph Intelligence (3 weeks) - Advanced relationship detection and knowledge graphs
• Advanced Search Intelligence (2 weeks) - Query expansion, fuzzy matching, multi-dimensional scoring  
• Rich Memory Types & Templates (2 weeks) - JSON, files, attachments, predefined templates
• Batch Operations & Workflows (1 week) - Bulk processing and automated workflows

PHASE 2 HIGHLIGHTS (Weeks 9-16):
• Advanced Analytics & Insights (2 weeks) - Usage reports, pattern analysis, recommendations
• Performance & Scalability (3 weeks) - Vector databases, caching, optimization
• Multi-tenancy & Security (3 weeks) - Authentication, data isolation, encryption, compliance

PHASE 3 HIGHLIGHTS (Weeks 17-24):
• AI-Powered Memory Intelligence (4 weeks) - Predictive engine, memory evolution, conflict resolution
• Memory Conversation Interface (2 weeks) - Natural language chat with memories
• Ecosystem Integration Platform (2 weeks) - REST API, webhooks, Slack/Notion/GitHub integrations

IMMEDIATE NEXT STEPS:
1. Review and prioritize enhancement areas based on business needs
2. Select Phase 1 starting point (likely memory relationships or advanced search)
3. Set up development environment with required dependencies
4. Begin implementation with chosen feature area

content:
ControlAI MCP debugging solution - Updated MCP configuration to use local server for easier debugging

Problem: ControlAI MCP trying to connect to CBD service on port 4180 which wasn't running
Solution: 
1. Updated mcp.json to use local server: node e:\GitHub\codai-project\packages\controlai-mcp\dist\server.js
2. Started CBD service: cd packages\cbd && npm run service
3. Added debug environment variables for better logging

MCP config changed from npm package to local development server.
CBD service running at http://localhost:4180 but appears to have SIGINT handling issues.

Next steps: Test ControlAI MCP connection with local debugging setup

content:
ControlAI MCP CBD Auto-Start Fix - COMPLETED

Problem: ControlAI MCP auto-start function was using incorrect NPX command for CBD service
Root Cause: Code was using `npx @codai/cbd service` but correct command is `npx cbd-service`

Solution Applied:
1. Fixed DatabaseService.ts and DatabaseService_fixed.ts
2. Changed NPX command from `['@codai/cbd', 'service']` to `['cbd-service']`
3. Built and published controlai-mcp@2.1.4
4. Reverted MCP config to clean version (like MemorAI MCP)

MCP Config: Clean setup using `npx controlai-mcp@latest` - no manual paths needed
Auto-start: Should now work automatically when ControlAI MCP starts

Status: ✅ READY FOR TESTING

ControlAI MCP CBD Auto-Start Fix - COMPLETED

content: ControlAI MCP Environment Variables Fix - SUCCESS: Fixed AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY variable name in workspace-ai .env file. ControlAI MCP now initializes successfully with proper Azure OpenAI configuration. Auto-start logic works correctly - attempts CBD service startup and gracefully falls back to local persistent database when CBD fails. Server running on localhost:7001 with all 7 MCP tools registered. Environment variables properly loaded from E:\GitHub\workspace-ai\.env via DOTENV_CONFIG_PATH.
